tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: South Africa Fulfills Every Wish on Nine-Year-Old Zelnay's Bucket List in Just 12 Hours
stories.summary: A community rallied to grant every single dream on a brave girl's bucket list, turning wishes into cherished memories through collective love.

stories.headline: Firefighters and Town Rally to Rescue Student's Graduation Dress, Saving the Day
stories.summary: When chaos threatened a graduation, firefighters and strangers united to retrieve a precious dress and turn the moment into pure celebration.

stories.headline: Golden Retriever Opens a 'Kissing Booth' and Spreads Free Joy to the Whole Neighborhood
stories.summary: A kind woman shares her golden retriever's friendly licks and warmth with neighbors through her window—free cuddles and smiles every single day.

stories.headline: Pigs Walk Again: Scientists Restore Movement with Experimental Spinal Cord Gel
stories.summary: Scientist Michael Lebenstein-Gumovski's fusogen-based gel reconnected severed spinal cords in pigs, restoring their ability to walk within two months—offering profound hope for human patients.

stories.headline: Against the Odds: Kordofan Giraffes Make Stunning Comeback in Congo
stories.summary: Garamba National Park's Kordofan giraffes surged from 22 in 2012 to 105 today, with zero poaching since 2017—a stunning conservation triumph for Africa's most endangered subspecies.

stories.headline: Community Gives $3,000 Bay Window to Family With Neurodivergent Children
stories.summary: KT Fairglass donated a $3,000 bay window to Gordon Passmore's family on 43rd Street, transforming a drafty, heat-losing home into a warmer, brighter space for his neurodivergent children.

stories.headline: Critically Endangered Marmots Return to the Wild as Population Soars from 30 to 400
stories.summary: Four Vancouver Island marmots released on Mount Washington mark a stunning conservation triumph—a breeding program brought the critically endangered species back from 30 to 400.

stories.headline: Curaçao's Goalkeeper Delivers Historic First World Cup Point in Stunning Debut
stories.summary: Debutant nation Curaçao secured their first-ever World Cup point with a goalless draw, anchored by keeper Eloy Room's sensational, heart-stopping performance.

stories.headline: Scientists Create 'Nanotrap' That Captures HIV and Awakens the Body's Defenses
stories.summary: Researchers from Drexel University and University of Pennsylvania engineered a 'nanotrap' that disguises itself as an immune cell to lure, trap, and awaken the body's defenses against HIV.

stories.headline: Battery-Free Device Turns Sunlight into Fuel That Powers Itself
stories.summary: Osaka scientists created an artificial photosynthesis system that harvests solar energy to convert water and CO₂ into fuel, with self-regulation built right in.

stories.headline: India's Homegrown Antibiotic Gets FDA Nod After 17 Years—a New Hope Against Superbugs
stories.summary: After 17 years of development, Wockhardt's ZAYNICH antibiotic won FDA approval—India's first fully homegrown drug discovery achieving this milestone, offering powerful hope against drug-resistant bacteria.
